From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Scottish trade union leader

| Gary Smith | |
| --- | --- |
| Gary Smith addresses GMB Congress in June 2021, following his election as General Secretary. | |
|  | |
| General Secretary of [GMB](/wiki/GMB_(trade_union) "GMB (trade union)") | |
| **[Incumbent](/wiki/Incumbent "Incumbent")** | |
| **Assumed office**  June 2021 | |
|  | |
|  | |

**Gary Smith** (born 1965) is a Scottish [trade union](/wiki/Trade_union "Trade union") leader.

Born in [Edinburgh](/wiki/Edinburgh "Edinburgh"),[[1]](#cite_note-1) Smith completed an apprenticeship as a gas fitter, joining the [GMB](/wiki/GMB_(trade_union) "GMB (trade union)") at the age of 16. He became a [shop steward](/wiki/Shop_steward "Shop steward") and when he was 26 the union paid for him to study at [Ruskin College](/wiki/Ruskin_College "Ruskin College"), which he followed with a master's degree in industrial relations from [Warwick University](/wiki/Warwick_University "Warwick University").[[2]](#cite_note-evans-2)[[3]](#cite_note-bbc-3)

After completing his education, Smith returned to working in the gas industry, and to trade union activism. He began working full-time for the union, as Recruitment Officer, then as a Senior Organiser, National Officer, and then as National Secretary for Commercial Services. In 2015, he became the union's Scotland Secretary.[[2]](#cite_note-evans-2) In this role, he was frequently critical of the [Scottish National Party](/wiki/Scottish_National_Party "Scottish National Party") government.[[4]](#cite_note-4) A supporter of [Keir Starmer](/wiki/Keir_Starmer "Keir Starmer")'s leadership of the [Labour Party](/wiki/Labour_Party_(UK) "Labour Party (UK)"), he opposed [Richard Leonard](/wiki/Richard_Leonard "Richard Leonard")'s efforts to remain leader of [Scottish Labour](/wiki/Scottish_Labour "Scottish Labour"), despite Leonard being a member of the GMB.[[3]](#cite_note-bbc-3)

In 2021, Smith was elected as General Secretary of the union, defeating Rehana Azam and Giovanna Holt. As General Secretary, he pledged to implement the recommendations of a report by [Karon Monaghan QC](/wiki/Karon_Monaghan "Karon Monaghan"), which argued that the union had problems with "bullying, misogyny, cronyism and sexual harassment".[[3]](#cite_note-bbc-3)

Smith urged the Labour Party to back [fracking](/wiki/Fracking "Fracking") in September 2022, telling them not to bow to the "bourgeois environmental lobby".[[5]](#cite_note-5) In August 2023, Smith criticised the expansion of the London [Ultra-Low Emissions Zone](/wiki/Ultra_Low_Emission_Zone "Ultra Low Emission Zone") and called for the Labour Party to rethink its plans to ban new oil and gas exploration in the North Sea.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

In June 2024 the *[New Statesman](/wiki/New_Statesman "New Statesman")* named Smith 46th in *The Left Power List 2024*, the magazine's "guide to the 50 most influential people in progressive politics".[[7]](#cite_note-7)

2. ^ [***a***](#cite_ref-evans_2-0) [***b***](#cite_ref-evans_2-1) Evans, Rima (17 March 2021). ["Gary Smith, GMB Union Scotland Secretary"](https://www.irguru.co.uk/ir-guru-interview/gary-smith-gmb-scotland-secretary/). *IR Guru*. Retrieved 8 June 2021.
3. ^ [***a***](#cite_ref-bbc_3-0) [***b***](#cite_ref-bbc_3-1) [***c***](#cite_ref-bbc_3-2) ["GMB: Union elects Gary Smith as general secretary"](https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/uk-politics-57343820). *BBC News*. 3 June 2021. Retrieved 8 June 2021.
4. **[^](#cite_ref-4)** McCall, Chris (3 June 2021). ["Scottish trade union veteran Gary Smith elected general secretary of GMB"](https://www.dailyrecord.co.uk/news/politics/scottish-trade-union-veteran-gary-24243830). *Daily Record*. Retrieved 8 June 2021.
5. **[^](#cite_ref-5)** Wearmouth, Rachel (22 September 2022). ["Labour must "face reality" on the energy crisis and back fracking, says GMB boss"](https://www.newstatesman.com/politics/labour/2022/09/labour-fracking-energy-crisis-gmb-union). *New Statesman*. Retrieved 22 September 2022.
6. **[^](#cite_ref-6)** ["GMB boss warns Keir Starmer not to risk 'economic destruction'"](https://www.express.co.uk/news/politics/1803940/gmb-gary-smith-keir-starmer-ulez-net-zero). *Express.co.uk*. 19 August 2023. Retrieved 23 August 2023.
7. **[^](#cite_ref-7)** ["The Left Power List 2024"](https://www.newstatesman.com/politics/uk-politics/2024/06/new-statesman-left-power-list-2024). *[New Statesman](/wiki/New_Statesman "New Statesman")*. 4 June 2024. Retrieved 9 October 2024.
